name: v0.60.0
body:
This version of `mabel` requires Bazel 7+ and will only work with `bzlmod`.

### Installation

To use this version, add the following to your `MODULE.bazel` file:

```starlark
bazel_dep(name = "mabel", version = "0.60.0")

# Optional: Use the release archive directly until it is available in the BCR
archive_override(
    module_name = "mabel",
    urls = ["https://github.com/menny/mabel/releases/download/v0.60.0/mabel-0.60.0.tar.gz"],
    integrity = "sha256-jaJvtrPwBTfuOnJiwwl68/W7IgLWiBy5OwifnZwt3Xs=",
    strip_prefix = "mabel-0.60.0",
)
```

## Setup
To add dependencies, define those in a `BUILD.bazel` file:
```starlark
load("@mabel//rules:mabel.bzl", "artifact", "mabel_rule")

mabel_rule(
    name = "mvn_deps",
    lockfile_path = " mvn_install.json",
    maven_deps = [
        artifact("com.google.guava:guava:20.0"),
    ],
)
```

Then in your MODULE.bazel file:
```starlark
mabel = use_extension("@mabel//rules:extensions.bzl", "mabel")
mabel.install(
    aliases_repo = "mvn",
    lockfile = "//:mvn_install.json",
)
use_repo(mabel, "mvn")
```

Read more in the [README](https://github.com/menny/mabel#usage).


## What's Changed
* Update mabel version to 0.50.2 by @github-actions[bot] in https://github.com/menny/mabel/pull/123
* Support for bazel 9 by @menny in https://github.com/menny/mabel/pull/124


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/menny/mabel/compare/v0.50.2...v0.60.0

This version of `mabel` requires Bazel 7+ and will only work with `bzlmod`.

### Installation

To use this version, add the following to your `MODULE.bazel` file:

# Optional: Use the release archive directly until it is available in the BCR
archive_override(
    module_name = "mabel",
    urls = ["https://github.com/menny/mabel/releases/download/v0.50.2/mabel-0.50.2.tar.gz"],
    integrity = "sha256-KXNS6+Mwi/NPh+X+RikjCLpfxuFmbqC5tKu+Ms8ooBg=",
    strip_prefix = "mabel-0.50.2",
)
```

## Setup
To add dependencies, define those in a `BUILD.bazel` file:

    name = "mvn_deps",

    ],

```

Then in your MODULE.bazel file:
```starlark
mabel = use_extension("@mabel//rules:extensions.bzl", "mabel")
mabel.install(
    aliases_repo = "mvn",
    lockfile = "//:mvn_install.json",
)
use_repo(mabel, "mvn")
```

Read more in the [README](https://github.com/menny/mabel#usage).


## What's Changed
* [LLM] Mark rules_multitool and aspect_rules_lint as dev-dependencies by @menny in https://github.com/menny/mabel/pull/122


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/menny/mabel/compare/v0.50.1...v0.50.2

This version of `mabel` requires Bazel 7+ and will only work with `bzlmod`.

### Installation

To use this version, add the following to your `MODULE.bazel` file:

```starlark
bazel_dep(name = "mabel", version = "0.50.1")

# Optional: Use the release archive directly until it is available in the BCR
archive_override(
    module_name = "mabel",
    urls = ["https://github.com/menny/mabel/releases/download/v0.50.1/mabel-0.50.1.tar.gz"],
    integrity = "sha256-8tnSo+Ewv8cKchLesgv//hJP/IC4G4zNzlE/ZnzB9UE=",

```

## Setup
In your `MODULE.bazel` file:
  ```starlark
  bazel_dep(name = "mabel", version = "0.50.1")
  ```

  To add dependencies, define those in a `BUILD.bazel` file:
  ```starlark
  load("@mabel//rules:mabel.bzl", "artifact", "mabel_rule")

  mabel_rule(
      name = "mvn_deps",
      lockfile_path = " mvn_install.json",
      maven_deps = [
          artifact("com.google.guava:guava:20.0"),
      ],
  )
  ```

  Then in your MODULE.bazel file:
  ```starlark
  mabel = use_extension("@mabel//rules:extensions.bzl", "mabel")
  mabel.install(
      aliases_repo = "mvn",
      lockfile = "//:mvn_install.json",
  )
  use_repo(mabel, "mvn")
  ```

  Read more in the [README](https://github.com/menny/mabel#usage).


## What's Changed
* Update mabel version to 0.50.0 by @github-actions[bot] in https://github.com/menny/mabel/pull/119
* Initial BCR support by @menny in https://github.com/menny/mabel/pull/120

## New Contributors
* @github-actions[bot] made their first contribution in https://github.com/menny/mabel/pull/119

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/menny/mabel/compare/v0.50.0...v0.50.1

This version of `mabel` requires Bazel 7+ and will only work with `bzlmod`.
## Setup
In your `MODULE.bazel` file:
  ```starlark
  bazel_dep(name = "mabel", version = "0.50.0")
  ```

  To add dependencies, define those in a `BUILD.bazel` file:
  ```starlark
  load("@mabel//rules:mabel.bzl", "artifact", "mabel_rule")

  mabel_rule(
      name = "mvn_deps",
      lockfile_path = " mvn_install.json",
      maven_deps = [
          artifact("com.google.guava:guava:20.0"),
      ],
  )
  ```

  Then in your MODULE.bazel file:
  ```starlark
  mabel = use_extension("@mabel//rules:extensions.bzl", "mabel")
  mabel.install(
      aliases_repo = "mvn",
      lockfile = "//:mvn_install.json",
  )
  use_repo(mabel, "mvn")
  ```

  Read more in the [README](https://github.com/menny/mabel#usage).


## What's Changed
* Update release workflow to create PR instead of direct push by @google-labs-jules[bot] in https://github.com/menny/mabel/pull/94
* [LLM] Add unit tests for artifact macro in mabel.bzl by @google-labs-jules[bot] in https://github.com/menny/mabel/pull/95
* Use Cassandra for ai review by @menny in https://github.com/menny/mabel/pull/113
* [LLM] Modernize AI Code Review workflow by @menny in https://github.com/menny/mabel/pull/114
* ⚡ Bolt: Parallelize source JAR resolution by @google-labs-jules[bot] in https://github.com/menny/mabel/pull/112
* ⚡ Bolt: Optimize JAR inspection in RuleClassifiers by @google-labs-jules[bot] in https://github.com/menny/mabel/pull/106
* [LLM] ⚡ Comprehensive performance optimizations by @menny in https://github.com/menny/mabel/pull/115
* [LLM] ⚡ Improve thread-safety and performance in timing and builders by @menny in https://github.com/menny/mabel/pull/116
* [LLM] Enable Android support and modernize examples by @menny in https://github.com/menny/mabel/pull/118


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/menny/mabel/compare/v0.40.1...v0.50.0

name: v0.40.1
body:
This is a big rewrite of the starlark part in order to achieve a clean and complete `bzlmod` support. As a result:
- only supporting `bzlmod` setups
- `android` is not yet supported due to an issue with [`rules_android`](https://github.com/bazelbuild/rules_android/issues/410)
This version of `mabel` requires Bazel 7+ and will only work with `bzlmod`.
## Setup
In your `MODULE.bazel` file:
  ```starlark
  bazel_dep(name = "mabel", version = "0.40.1")
  ```

  To add dependencies, define those in a `BUILD.bazel` file:
  ```starlark
  load("@mabel//rules:mabel.bzl", "artifact", "mabel_rule")

  mabel_rule(
      name = "mvn_deps",
      lockfile_path = " mvn_install.json",
      maven_deps = [
          artifact("com.google.guava:guava:20.0"),
      ],
  )
  ```

  Then in your MODULE.bazel file:
  ```starlark
  mabel = use_extension("@mabel//rules:extensions.bzl", "mabel")
  mabel.install(
      aliases_repo = "mvn",
      lockfile = "//:mvn_install.json",
  )
  use_repo(mabel, "mvn")
  ```

  Read more in the [README](https://github.com/menny/mabel#usage).


## What's Changed
* Move to bzlmod by @menny in https://github.com/menny/mabel/pull/91
* [LLM] Add GitHub Release workflow by @google-labs-jules[bot] in https://github.com/menny/mabel/pull/92

## New Contributors
* @google-labs-jules[bot] made their first contribution in https://github.com/menny/mabel/pull/92

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/menny/mabel/compare/0.30.0...v0.40.1

name: v0.30.0
body:
* Updated to support Bazel 6.1.0.
* Output now conforms to the latest `buildifier`.
* Updated `examples` to latest rules.

name: v0.20.0
body: * Using `jvm_import` instead of `java_import` and `kt_*`

name: v0.19.1
body: * You can now also set `exports` type on specific artifact.

name: v0.19.0
body:
* You can now specify how we generate `exports` attribute:
  * `all` - Generate `exports` for all resolved dependencies.
  * `requested_deps` - (the default) Generate only for top-level, requested, artifacts.
  * `none` - do not generate `exports` attribute in any of the targets.

name: v0.18.1
body: * Fix for test-only marking of inner-deps.

name: v0.18.0
body: * Support for `test_only` artifacts  - such artifacts will be marked as test-only and Bazel will not allow those to be used in production code.

name: v0.17.0
body: * Support for artifacts' target type hint. This may speed up resolution quick a bit for large repos.

name: v0.16.3
body: * Fix for `java_plugin#runtime_dep` mapping

name: v.0.16.2
body: - fix for null licenses

name: v.0.16.1
body:
* Correctly resolve dependencies with a profile-activation mechanism.
* Pass `JDK-HOME` path for local depdencies.

name: v0.16.0
body:
* Annotating targets with the reason they were created:
  * Root requested dependency.
  * Required dependency by another dependency.
* Annotating targets with license information:
  * Name as shown in the pom file.
  * Type (Apache, MIT, etc).
  * URL to the license file.
* Failing if a root dependency was not found.

name: v0.15.0
body:
* Support for `kotlin-rules` 1.5.0-alpha3
* Verified with Bazel 4.0.0

name: v.0.14.3
body: * Fix for Kotlin jars that also contain java_plugin.

name: v0.14.2
body: * Fixed typo in generated files.

name: v0.14.1
body: * Better `buildifier` output.

name: v0.14.0
body: * Other than java-rules, all other rules must be provided. For example, if a dependency is an Android dependency and `aar_import` was not provided to the init macro (`generate_transitive_dependency_targets`) then the build will fail with an error message.

name: v0.13.2
body: * Support for java-plugins (annotation processors) with comments in their manifest.

name: v0.13.1
body:
* Verified with Bazel 3.3.1.
* Fix an issue with a specific malformed version structure.
* Avoid cyclic-dependency issues.

name: v0.13.0
body: * Support for updated kotlin_rules.

name: v0.12.0
body:
* Ability to provide a `java_library` and `java_plugin` rule implementations.
* Generated `BUILD.bazel` files are compliant to `buildifier`.

name: v0.11.0
body:
* Rules and `bzl` files were renamed to include `mabel`.
* You can now specify which version-conflict resolution strategy will be used. Use the `version_conflict_resolver` attribute
* A new version-conflict resolution strategy: `latest-version`. This is the default.

name: v0.10.2
body: * Fix for missing maven-coordinates tag

name: v0.10.1
body:
* Fix an issue with Kotlin rules and visibility
* Working with Bazel 3.1.0

name: v0.10.0
body: * Ability to specify the visibility of generated targets, according to a category.

name: v0.9.2
body: * Option to keep files in the target folder.
